working-tree-staging-and-history.label: Record the First Community Site Snapshot
working-tree-staging-and-history.summary: Carry the staged community-site proposal into its first commit, then verify history and clean state.

sketch2.title: Verify History and Current State Separately

try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.title: Record the First Community Site Snapshot
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.prompt: The five supplied `community-site/` files are already staged from the previous milestone. Type `git commit -m "Start community site"` and press Enter.
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.hint: Record the proposed snapshot with the exact authored message.

help.concept: A commit records the staged snapshot as one named point in local history.
help.hint_1: The message is Start community site.
help.hint_2: Do not add or edit files during this step.

try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.instructions: The five supplied `community-site/` files are already staged from the previous milestone. Type `git commit -m "Start community site"` and press Enter.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you run `git commit -m "Start community site"`?

try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.title: Read the First History Entry
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.prompt: The first commit now exists. Type `git log --oneline` and press Enter. Confirm the newest entry says `Start community site`.
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.hint: Read the concise history without changing it.

help.concept: git log --oneline presents each recorded snapshot as a short identifier and subject.
help.hint_1: The exact hash is not graded.
help.hint_2: The commit subject should be Start community site.

try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.starterCode:
# Community Site

Local events and resources.
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.instructions: The first commit now exists. Type `git log --oneline` and press Enter. Confirm the newest entry says `Start community site`.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site'

#!/usr/bin/env bash set -eu rm -rf -- community-site mkdir -p -- community-site git -C community-site init -q -b main git -C community-site config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner' git -C community-site config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s '# Community Site Local events and resources. ' > community-site/README.md mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s ' Community Site

Community Events

' > community-site/index.html mkdir -p -- community-site printf %s 'body { font-family: sans-serif; } footer { margin-top: 12px; } ' > community-site/styles.css mkdir -p -- community-site/notes printf %s 'Archive the spring event list. ' > community-site/notes/old-events.txt mkdir -p -- community-site/dist printf %s 'console.log('"'"'legacy preview'"'"'); ' > community-site/dist/legacy-preview.js git -C community-site add -A GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C community-site commit -q -m 'Start community site'

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.

try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.title: Verify the First Clean State
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.prompt: The same first commit remains checked out. Type `git status --short` and press Enter. A clean short status prints no file lines.
try_working_tree_staging_and_history_ske.hint: Use the compact status view to verify that no unfinished project work remains.

help.concept: A clean working tree means the visible tracked files match the current committed snapshot and nothing is staged.
help.hint_1: Do not expect a success sentence from the command.
help.hint_2: No file lines is the expected result.

single-choice-1.title: Identify the Working Tree
single-choice-1.prompt: What is the working tree in Git?
single-choice-1.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Follow one edit from the working tree to the staging area and into a named commit, checking each boundary along the way.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: The project files and folders you can currently edit
options.b: The proposed snapshot prepared for the next commit
options.d: The list of patterns Git should ignore

single-choice-2.title: Purpose of the Staging Area
single-choice-2.prompt: What is the purpose of the staging area in Git?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Follow one edit from the working tree to the staging area and into a named commit, checking each boundary along the way.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

options.a: To prepare selected changes for the next commit
options.b: To store the complete commit history
options.c: To display only unstaged line changes

multi-choice-1.title: What a Commit Records
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which facts about a commit are correct? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Follow one edit from the working tree to the staging area and into a named commit, checking each boundary along the way.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

options.b: It includes a message and author identity.
options.c: It automatically includes every unstaged edit.
options.d: It becomes part of repository history.

drag-reorder-1.title: Order of Git Workflow
drag-reorder-1.prompt: Arrange the steps of the Git workflow in the correct order.
drag-reorder-1.hint: Start with the state that must exist before the next action can make sense.

help.concept: Follow one edit from the working tree to the staging area and into a named commit, checking each boundary along the way.
help.hint_1: Track how the project moves from working files to a proposed snapshot and then to history.
help.hint_2: Read the ordered steps as one continuous repository story.

tokens.t1: Edit files in the working tree
tokens.t2: Stage changes in the staging area
tokens.t3: Commit changes to the commit history

working-tree-intro.title: The Proposal Carries Forward
working-tree-intro.bodyMarkdown: The five supplied files arrive already staged from the previous topic. Nothing is restaged or recreated; the same proposed snapshot now moves into history.

staging-area-intro.title: A Commit Records the Proposal
staging-area-intro.bodyMarkdown: `git commit -m "Start community site"` records the staged snapshot as one point in local history.

commit-history-intro.title: Verify History and Current State Separately
commit-history-intro.bodyMarkdown: `git log --oneline` confirms what was recorded. `git status --short` separately confirms that no unfinished project work remains.
